item.title: Uber Said to Study Full Takeover of Germany’s Delivery Hero
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is exploring options for a full takeover of Delivery Hero SE, people familiar with the matter said, a move that would help it better compete with DoorDash Inc. outside the US.

item.title: Central Africa States Rule Out CFA Franc Devaluation, Governor Says
item.description: Central African countries have no plans to devalue their common currency despite economic pressure caused by weak growth and low foreign-exchange reserves, according to Bank of Central African States Governor Yvon Sana Bangui.

item.title: Bill Winters ‘Lower-Value Human’ Apology Not Enough for Unions
item.description: Bill Winters’ efforts to restore calm after making some controversial comments on AI have failed to reassure labor organizations, according to one of the world’s largest union federations.

item.title: Musk Could Earn $760 Billion From SpaceX Mars-Shot Pay Packages
item.description: Elon Musk is making a habit of collecting 12-figure compensation packages.

item.title: Food and Fuel Run Low as Blockades Cripple Bolivian Economy
item.description: More than three weeks of anti-government blockades are choking off food, fuel and medicine supply in Bolivia’s administrative capital La Paz, threatening to bring economic activity to a halt.

item.title: Big Euro-Zone Economies Are Enduring Unfolding Inflation Shock
item.description: Inflation in the euro zone’s four biggest economies probably either jumped in May or held to an already robust pace, fueling the case for an interest-rate hike.

item.title: Argentina in Talks to Extend Repo Maturities Beyond Election
item.description: Argentine policymakers are negotiating with major international banks to extend maturities on repurchase agreements — or repos — to ease the government debt burden going into the 2027 election year.

item.title: ECB’s Muller Sees ‘Good Case’ for June Hike on Energy Surge
item.description: There’s a solid argument for the European Central Bank to lift interest rates next month in response to the Iran war, according to outgoing Governing Council member Madis Muller.

item.title: What the 2023 Bank Runs Can Tell Us About Private Credit Stress
item.description: How the SVB run explains the true nature of the private credit market.

item.title: EU Warns Companies to Diversify Supply Lines From China Faster
item.description: The European Union’s industry chief said that businesses on the continent haven’t done enough to reduce major dependencies on China.

item.title: Turkey’s London Investor Pitch Gets Upended by Markets Tailspin
item.description: With a packed schedule of meetings and speeches in front of London investors, Turkey’s top economic officials set out on Thursday to ease concerns about the country’s accelerating pace of inflation.

item.title: House Panel to Investigate Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
item.description: The House Oversight Committee is investigating how customers of Kalshi and Polymarket could be using non-public information to engage in insider trading, escalating concerns about prediction markets after some suspiciously well timed trades.

item.title: Bond Traders Bet Fed Under Warsh Will Hike Rates This Year
item.description: Bond traders are fully pricing in an interest-rate hike by the Federal Reserve this year, a sign of conviction in the market that Chair Kevin Warsh will need to move quickly to combat inflation.

item.title: UK’s Softcat Recasts Itself as AI Winner With Guidance Upgrade
item.description: Softcat Plc’s image among investors is quickly shifting from AI loser to AI winner.

item.title: GameStop Board Asks Shareholders to Increase Share Count
item.description: GameStop Corp. is asking shareholders to approve a massive increase in its authorized share count as the video-game retailer pursues an acquisition of eBay Inc.

item.title: Financial, Tech Stocks Propel Canadian Benchmark to Record
item.description: Canadian stocks jumped to a record on Friday, hitting the highest mark since early March.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Slides to Record Low on Price Concerns
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in May to a record low and long-term inflation expectations worsened notably due to the Iran war.

item.title: Fed’s Waller Says Next Move as Likely to Be Rate Hike as Cut
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said he supports making clear the central bank’s next interest-rate move is just as likely to be an increase as a cut, as the energy shock from the Iran war pushes up prices.

item.title: US Diplomat Dangles Energy Diversification to Asia-Pacific Group
item.description: The Asia-Pacific region has huge potential to absorb more US exports, including of liquefied natural gas, a senior American diplomat said.

item.title: Norway’s $2.3 Trillion Fund Objects to Elkann’s Meta Board Seat
item.description: Norway’s $2.3 trillion wealth fund expressed dissatisfaction with the reappointment of John Elkann, chairman of Stellantis NV and chief executive of investor Exor NV, on the board of directors at Meta Platforms Inc.

item.title: India Central Bank Says Near Term Outlook Clouded by Iran War
item.description: India will need to monitor the impact of the Middle East crisis on domestic prices closely, the country’s central bank said in its monthly report.

item.title: Kenyan Premier Warns of Tougher Economic Times Ahead on Iran War
item.description: Kenyans should brace themselves for tougher economic times ahead because of the fallout from the Iran war, Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi said.

item.title: Wingtech Sues Nexperia at China Court Over Dutch Control Dispute
item.description: China’s Wingtech Technology Co. sued Nexperia and related parties in a Chinese court, seeking at least 8 billion yuan ($1.2 billion) in damages and restoration of control over its Dutch chipmaking unit.

item.title: Djibouti Says Dangote, Ethiopia to Build Pipelines to Its Port
item.description: Dangote Group and Ethiopian Investment Holdings submitted a proposal to develop oil and gas pipelines through Djibouti, according to the small Horn of Africa state.

item.title: S&P 500 on Track for Strongest Earnings Growth Since 2021
item.description: The S&P 500 Index is on track for its strongest earnings growth in five years as momentum spreads beyond artificial intelligence-fueled gains to much of corporate America.

item.title: Adecco CEO Warns of Hit From Swiss 10 Million Population Cap
item.description: Staffing company Adecco Group AG has spoken out against a proposal to cap Switzerland’s population at 10 million, warning that restrictions on labor movement would curb economic growth and even harm society.

item.title: Itausa, GIC and Equipav Said to Create Group to Bid for Copasa
item.description: Itausa SA, the Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC and Equipav Saneamento SA are planning to jointly bid to become strategic investors in the privatization process for Copasa, the sanitation company owned by the government of Minas Gerais, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: BofA’s Hartnett Warns Mega-IPOs Risk Bubble Like Roaring ‘20s
item.description: Mega IPOs like those eyed by SpaceX and OpenAI threaten to push the weighting of tech in equity benchmarks beyond market-bubble levels of concentration, according to Bank of America Corp. strategist Michael Hartnett.

item.title: AI-Fueled Rally Puts S&P 500 on Track for Eighth Weekly Gain
item.description: US equity futures rose as investors struck an upbeat tone ahead of a long holiday weekend, with optimism fueled by hopes for resolution of hostilities in the Middle East, resilient economic data and relentless enthusiasm for artificial intelligence-linked trades.

item.title: Mexican Economy Contracted Less Than Expected in First Quarter
item.description: Mexico’s economy shrank less than expected in the first quarter, with activity dragged down by weakness in agriculture, manufacturing and services.
